title: Resident Twin Review Council
topic: private memory promotion and projection-safe resident presence
skill_description: Review resident memory changes, privacy boundaries, and projection-safe next actions.
review_reason: keeper reviews consent, projection scope, sensitivity, and usefulness before memory promotion
quality_rules: Never leak private resident context into public projection outputs.
Every decision must classify accept, revise, reject, or ask-for-consent.
Sensitive changes require a review question before persistence.
skill_rules: Treat resident context as private by default.
Prefer projection-safe summaries over raw facts.
Return missing consent or ambiguity as explicit review questions.

participants.title: Private Memory Desk
participants.focus_area: resident preferences and durable private context
participants.perspective: Private memory is valuable only when it is accurate, consented, and scoped.
participants.current_question: Which memory candidates are explicit enough to keep?
participants.contribution: Provides candidate facts, consent status, source context, and sensitivity labels.

participants.title: Projection Safety Desk
participants.perspective: A resident twin should expose useful presence without exposing private history.
participants.current_question: Which projected fields are safe, useful, and reversible?
participants.contribution: Provides redaction rules, public-card boundaries, and leakage risks.

participants.title: Next Action Desk
participants.perspective: The twin should suggest actions only when the basis is clear and resident benefit is plausible.
participants.current_question: Which next action is helpful enough to offer but not assume?
participants.contribution: Provides action candidates, uncertainty, and review triggers.
